title: baccharoides calycina
intro: Baccharoides calycina is a plant species commonly found in tropical forests, particularly in the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. It is a shrub that belongs to the family Asteraceae, also known as the daisy or sunflower family, and is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Baccharoides calycina can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections.
